memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159
memoryArtifacts.description: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius) in History of Monmouthshire, pgs. 116-118 [but missing the generation of Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer)]
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius) in History of Monmouthshire, pgs. 116-118 [but missing the generation of Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer)] skipping to Grandson Herbert FitzHerbert (FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert FitzHerbert.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert FitzHerbert and Lucy FitzWalter in Archaeologica Cambrensis, Third Series, No. XIII, January 1858, pgs. 23-26
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert FitzHerbert and Lucy FitzWalter in Archaeologica Cambrensis, Third Series, No. XIII, January 1858, pgs. 23-26

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert (Herbertus Camerarius) in Burke's Dormant, Abeyant, Forfeited and Extinct Peerages, pg. 271
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert (Herbertus Camerarius) in Burke's Dormant, Abeyant, Forfeited and Extinct Peerages, pg. 271. It is noted that this record skips the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert, and goes to Herbert's grandson (called his son), also named Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry or Herbert FitzHerbert I).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Forz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish: possibly an Anglicized (quasi-translation) form of Irish Gaelic Ó Gabhlaigh ‘descendant of Gabhalach’, from gabhlach ‘forked’. See Forkin .

English: from Middle English forke ‘fork, pitchfork’ (Old English force, forca), perhaps for an agricultural laborer or someone who worked with a pitchfork (see Forker ). Alternatively, through metathesis of -ro- to -or- the name may derive from Middle English frok ‘frock, outer garment’ (Old French froc ‘man's outer garment’), perhaps for a maker of clothes.

variants.name: Forkin
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form (quasi-translation) of Gaelic Ó Gabhláin, a name from a diminutive of gabhal ‘fork’. See Fork 1.
North German: possibly a nickname for a hog farmer or an unkempt person, from Middle Low German verken ‘piglet’.

variants.name: Forck
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Fork  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Forz was born about 1222, in England, his father, Hugh de Vivonne, was 29 and his mother, Mabel Malet, was 28. He married Maud de Ferrers on 30 July 1248, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died before 22 May 1259, in Somerset, England, and was buried in Meaux, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Forz est né environ 1222, à Angleterre, son père, Hugh de Vivonne, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Mabel Malet, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Ferrers le 30 juillet 1248, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 22 mai 1259, à Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Meaux, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Forzが約1222年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Hugh de Vivonneは29歳，母Mabel Maletは28歳であった。1248年7月30日にイギリスソメルセトでMaud de Ferrersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1259年5月22日以前にイギリスソメルセトで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアメアウックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Forz(이)가 대략 1222년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Vivonne(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabel Malet(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1248년 7월 30일 영국 서머세트에서 Maud de Ferrers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1259년 5월 22일 이전 영국 서머세트에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 모스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Forz родился около 1222 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh de Vivonne было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mabel Malet, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Ferrers 30 июля 1248 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер перед 22 май 1259 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия и был похоронен в местности мокс, еркшир, Англия.
